name: ukraine2015_blackout
description: Models the December 2015 attack on the Ukrainian grid (Sandworm / BlackEnergy3), the first publicly confirmed cyberattack to cause a blackout. After stealing operator credentials, the adversary used the utility's own SCADA to open breakers at around thirty substations, then bricked HMIs and serial-to-Ethernet converters and ran a telephone denial-of-service so operators could not see or respond. This scenario reproduces that on the wire: a second (attacker) association appears, reads the whole object model, opens the breakers across the bay, fights the operators by re-issuing open commands, and finally takes a station's view offline. The grid is live (config/grid.json), so the breaker opens cause a real cascading blackout. ATT&CK for ICS: T0888 Remote System Information Discovery, T0801 Monitor Process State, T0855 Unauthorized Command Message, T0831 Manipulation of Control, T0813 Denial of Control, T0815 Denial of View.

timeline.note: normal operations; legitimate RTU telemetry is steady
attacker association reads the model: version, supported features, bilateral table
reads every indication point to map the live grid state before acting
operators try to re-close; the attacker re-sends open, denying control

timeline.label: malicious
timeline.note: HMI and serial converters bricked: operator view lost at the transformer station
blackout across the load island; operators blind
